hero.title: HPC and AI: Shaping the Future at USP
hero.subtitle: Distributed Systems and Concurrent Programming Laboratory - Cutting-edge research in high-performance computing, artificial intelligence, and distributed systems.
section.about.desc: LASDPC (Distributed Systems and Concurrent Programming Laboratory) is based at ICMC-USP, in São Carlos. Founded in 1998, it is a reference in research and innovation in HPC, AI, distributed systems, and cloud computing.
section.activeStudents.desc: Currently enrolled members of the lab.
section.alumni: People who have passed through here
section.alumni.desc: Former lab members, including students, faculty, researchers, and collaborators.
contact.name: Name
contact.address: ICMC-USP, Av. Trabalhador São-Carlense, 400 - São Carlos, SP, Brazil
reservation.title: Resource Reservation
history.title: History
history.subtitle: A brief timeline of LaSDPC's origins, evolution, and current research directions.
history.events.1990.title: Foundation of LaSDPC
history.events.1990.summary: LaSDPC was born in 1990 when professors returned to Brazil after their PhD programs abroad.
history.events.1990.p1: Professors Marcos Jose Santana and Regina Helena Carlucci Santana started LaSDPC activities with the collaboration of Eduardo Marques, Onofre Trindade Junior, Nivaldi Calonego Junior, and Edson dos Santos Moreira.
history.events.1990.p2: This initial team established the lab's core mission around distributed systems and concurrent programming at ICMC/USP.
history.events.1990.p3: From the start, the lab fostered collaboration and mentorship to grow a strong research community.
history.events.1990.imageCaption: LaSDPC at ICMC/USP - the lab's early roots (placeholder image).
history.events.gsdpc.title: LaSDPC & the GSDPC Group
history.events.gsdpc.summary: LaSDPC's birth intertwines with the Distributed Systems and Concurrent Programming Group (GSDPC).
history.events.gsdpc.p1: In historical perspective, the creation of LaSDPC is strongly connected to the creation of GSDPC at ICMC/USP.
history.events.gsdpc.p2: LaSDPC serves as the place where most of GSDPC's research is conducted.
history.events.gsdpc.p3: This relationship helped consolidate research efforts and build continuity across projects and generations.
history.events.gsdpc.imageCaption: LaSDPC as the research home for GSDPC activities (placeholder image).
history.events.evolution.title: Growth Through the 1990s
history.events.evolution.summary: The lab followed the rapid evolution of distributed systems throughout the 1990s.
history.events.evolution.p1: Research focused on the development and evaluation of distributed computer systems, parallel algorithms, concurrent environments, and computer networks.
history.events.evolution.p2: Topics included parallel computing techniques (advanced architectures and parallel programming), distributed simulations, and process scheduling.
history.events.evolution.p3: These foundations shaped the lab's long-term approach: strong systems engineering paired with rigorous evaluation.
history.events.evolution.imageCaption: Research directions broaden as distributed systems evolve (placeholder image).
history.events.training.title: Training Researchers & Alumni Impact
history.events.training.summary: LaSDPC has trained 130+ MSc and PhD students and supported many undergraduate research projects.
history.events.training.p1: Training human resources alongside high-quality scientific knowledge is one of the lab's distinguishing characteristics.
history.events.training.p2: Undergraduate scientific initiation projects have also played an important role in the lab's output and culture.
history.events.training.p3: Alumni work in both industry and academia, with national and international presence.
history.events.training.imageCaption: Building people and community has always been central to LaSDPC (placeholder image).
history.events.today.title: Today's Research Themes
history.events.today.summary: The lab continues evolving while keeping its foundations in distributed and parallel systems.
history.events.today.p1: Current themes include green computing, cloud computing, SOA, web services, and high-performance applications.
history.events.today.p2: We also research testing of parallel and distributed applications, performance evaluation, adaptive systems, wireless sensor networks, and mobile computing.
history.events.today.p3: Educational computing is another active direction, including learning objects and open educational resources.
history.events.today.imageCaption: Modern research themes hosted at LaSDPC (placeholder image).
timeline.2018: Integration with AI and deep learning
